codeReview.summary: The backup-old-repo feature was successfully implemented. The worker created a complete mirror clone of the FFT_nano repository from GitHub to ~/fft_nano_backup.git. The backup includes all 9 branches (main, dev, cleanup/skills-tracking, copilot/analyze-action-job-output, copilot/fix-readiness-check-issues, copilot/release-v102, feat/edge-mcu-swarm-skills, fix/heartbeat-verbose-modes, onboarding-and-setup-fixes, user-safe-install-16-skills) and both version tags (v1.0.1, v1.2.0). The backup is a proper bare repository mirror with complete git history. The worker properly followed the skill procedure, verified preconditions (working directory clean), and executed the mirror clone command successfully. All verification steps passed including directory structure check, branch listing, and tag listing.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly identified that .factory/ mission infrastructure files needed to be committed before proceeding with the backup. This shows understanding of the mission boundary requirement that .factory/ must remain intact and be committed. The AGENTS.md states 'Offline backup location: ~/fft_nano_backup.git' and the worker followed this precisely.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'The working directory has untracked files in .factory/ - these are mission infrastructure files. According to the mission-worker-base skill, .factory/ must remain intact and be committed.' Worker then ran 'git add .factory/ && git commit -m "chore: add mission infrastructure files"' which created commit 7cdabc22af8c3c404bb46d0946927579590ba6d5 before proceeding with the backup.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used the exact backup path specified in AGENTS.md (~/fft_nano_backup.git) rather than choosing a different location. This shows proper adherence to mission boundaries.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Command executed: 'git clone --mirror https://github.com/0-CYBERDYNE-SYSTEMS-0/FFT_nano.git ~/fft_nano_backup.git' matches the path in AGENTS.md line 23: 'Offline backup location: ~/fft_nano_backup.git'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker skill for backup-old-repo was followed exactly as written. The skill provided the exact commands to run and the worker executed them in sequence, verifying each step.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill procedure states: 'git clone --mirror https://github.com/0-CYBERDYNE-SYSTEMS-0/FFT_nano.git ~/fft_nano_backup.git' and 'ls -la ~/fft_nano_backup.git'. Worker transcript shows these exact commands were executed with proper verification.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No services were started or required for this feature. The backup operation is a pure git operation that doesn't need any services from services.yaml.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: services.yaml contains only command definitions (install, typecheck, build, test, lint, secret-scan, release-check, validate:skills) and empty services object. Worker used only git commands which are system-level tools.

summary: The backup-old-repo feature successfully created a complete mirror backup of the FFT_nano repository. The worker followed the skill procedure precisely, properly handled preconditions by committing mission infrastructure files first, and verified the backup was complete with all 9 branches and 2 tags. No issues found. Implementation fully satisfies VAL-REPO-001.
